overview.summary: The ShadowRay campaign, a group with basic skill levels, targets the energy sector with a multi-stage complex cyber attack. They begin with reconnaissance to identify potential targets, acquire infrastructure for phishing, gain initial access through phishing emails exploiting known vulnerabilities, establish persistence with web shells, escalate privileges, evade defenses, steal credentials, discover network layouts, move laterally, collect sensitive data, exfiltrate it over command and control channels, and finally impact operations by disrupting critical services.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target Artifact (Sites), Application Log, Credential Store, File, Windows Service
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email, Search Engines, Response Metadata, Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Web Shell, Application Log Content, File Access, Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, File Modification

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker searches open websites and domains to identify potential targets within the energy sector, gathering information to plan the attack.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker sets up phishing domains mimicking energy company suppliers to be used in subsequent phases of the attack.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2015-1187 (Suggested): Remote code execution via ping tool in certain devices
stage.description: The attacker sends crafted emails with a CVE-2023-48022 lure to employees, leading to exploitation and gaining initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2013-3897 (Suggested): Remote code execution via CDisplayPointer in IE
stage.description: Post-phishing success, the attacker executes malicious scripts to achieve further objectives.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain access, the attacker deploys a web shell on the compromised server.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker gains higher-level permissions once the initial foothold is established.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker impairs security tools to avoid detection and continue the operation undetected.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker steals credentials to facilitate lateral movement throughout the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker understands the network layout to plan lateral movement effectively.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves across the network using stolen credentials.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker gathers sensitive data before exfiltration.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The collected data is transferred to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts operations by stopping critical services.
